preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that places a premium on approach play and total strokes gained. The course rewards players who hit greens from distance and convert putts at a high rate. This event matters because it offers a strong field and solid betting markets before the FedExCup Playoffs.
preview.modelExplainer: SG Approach (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) carry the heaviest weights, followed by SG Putting (16.8%) and SG Around-the-Green (13.3%). Driving accuracy and bogey avoidance each sit at 3.9% and 3.0%, so ball-striking and short-game conversion outweigh raw distance. The model tilts toward players who post strong approach and putting numbers at this venue.
preview.pickApproach: We tier bets by model rank versus power rank, targeting value where the tournament model outranks the baseline power rank. Top-tier bets focus on the top five names with the largest positive gaps. Mid-tier and long-shot bets chase names ranked 6-20 that still show strong SG Approach or SG Putting marks relative to their power rank.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Model ranks Scheffler, Knapp, and Matsuyama highest for the 144-player field starting July 23.
article.introduction: The tournament model places Jake Knapp at rank 1 with 2.002 SG Total and Scottie Scheffler at rank 2 with 1.868 SG Total. TPC Twin Cities weights SG Approach at 25.8 percent and SG Total at 22.8 percent. Post-Open workload and FedExCup context are factored for the top 25 rows.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: The 3M Open model ranks the top 20 by combined SG Total and power rank. Jake Knapp leads at tournament rank 1 with 2.002 SG Total. Scottie Scheffler sits at tournament rank 2 with 1.868 SG Total and power rank 2.

sections.heading: Course Factors
sections.body: TPC Twin Cities assigns 25.8 percent weight to SG Approach and 11.4 percent to SG Off-the-Tee. SG Around-the-Green carries 13.3 percent weight and SG Putting 16.8 percent. Driving Accuracy is weighted at 3.9 percent.

sections.heading: Post-Open Workload and Motivation
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler logged six rounds in two weeks after a top-5 Open finish and holds FedExCup rank 1. Jake Knapp played four rounds and sits at FedExCup rank 39. Hideki Matsuyama logged four rounds and holds FedExCup rank 37.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler (rank 2) is a strong model-supported bet despite six-round workload. Hideki Matsuyama (rank 4) is a secondary value play with 1.197 SG Total. David Lipsky (rank 5) is a speculative long shot with 0.89 SG Total and zero rounds in two weeks. Maverick McNealy (rank 6) is a speculative long shot with 0.994 SG Total.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: Jake Knapp (rank 1) is a strong model-supported bet with 2.002 SG Total. Scottie Scheffler (rank 2) is a secondary value play with six-round workload. Hideki Matsuyama (rank 4) is a secondary value play with 1.197 SG Total. Rasmus Højgaard (rank 10) is a speculative long shot with 0.823 SG Total. Beau Hossler (rank 20) is a speculative long shot with 0.479 SG Total.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: David Lipsky (rank 5) is a secondary value play with 0.89 SG Total. Maverick McNealy (rank 6) is a secondary value play with 0.994 SG Total. Andrew Putnam (rank 8) is a speculative long shot with 0.479 SG Total. Sam Stevens (rank 9) is a speculative long shot with 0.649 SG Total. Jason Day (rank 11) is a speculative long shot with 0.74 SG Total. Gary Woodland (rank 15) is a speculative long shot with 0.682 SG Total. Christiaan Bezuidenhout (rank 16) is a speculative long shot with 0.463 SG Total.

sections.heading: Players to Approach Cautiously
sections.body: Austin Smotherman (rank 7) shows 0.874 SG Approach but -0.494 SG Putting. Kurt Kitayama (rank 12) logged eight rounds in two weeks with 0.474 SG Total. Jordan Smith (rank 17) logged eight rounds in two weeks with 0.465 SG Total.

article.conclusion: Strong model-supported bets: Jake Knapp outright and top-10. Secondary value plays: Scottie Scheffler outright and top-10, Hideki Matsuyama outright and top-10. Speculative long shots: David Lipsky outright, Maverick McNealy outright and top-10, Rasmus Højgaard top-10, Beau Hossler top-10.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.
